rust-output.title: Output
rust-output.concept: println! writes a line. In Rust, Output is easiest to learn by tracing the value flow in the example: where a value is created, which expression reads or transforms it, and what finally reaches stdout. Before editing the exercise, predict the output line by line so the syntax is tied to behavior.
rust-output.worked_example: The worked example shows Output in Rust with the smallest code shape that produces an observable result. Read it once for syntax, then trace the value from creation to the final output.
common_mistakes: Memorizing the example instead of explaining the value flow behind Output.
Changing the Rust starter before predicting the expected output.
Fixing punctuation while missing the Rust rule that Output is practicing.
self_check: What value exists immediately before the program prints or returns?
Which line uses the Rust syntax rule for Output?
If you could change only one part of the starter first, what would it be?
rust-output.exercise_prompt: Complete the Output exercise in Rust. First predict what the starter will output, then make the smallest useful edit. Do not paste the expected output as a hard-coded constant.
